name: Battle of Waterloo (1815)
description: Wellington's Anglo-Allied army defended the ridge of Mont-Saint-Jean against Napoleon's last major offensive effort.

historical_outcome.description: Wellington repulsed the Imperial Guard assault, triggering French collapse.

name: Battle of Austerlitz (1805)
description: Napoleon lured the Russo-Austrian Coalition into attacking his weakened right wing, then stormed the Pratzen Heights.

historical_outcome.description: Soult stormed the Pratzen Heights, splitting the Allied army.

name: Battle of Borodino (1812)
description: The bloodiest single day of the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon withheld his Guard; the Russians fell back in good order.

historical_outcome.description: Tactically indecisive. Both sides suffered catastrophic losses.

name: Battle of Jena (1806)
description: Napoleon pinned and destroyed Hohenlohe's Prussian force at Jena.

historical_outcome.description: Decisive French victory. The Prussian army was routed.

name: Battle of Eylau (1807)
description: A bloody winter battle in a blizzard. Murat's massive cavalry charge stabilised the French centre.

historical_outcome.description: Pyrrhic French tactical victory. Both armies suffered massive casualties in appalling winter conditions.

name: Battle of Friedland (1807)
description: Napoleon caught Bennigsen's Russian army with its back to the Alle River and crushed it, forcing Russia to seek peace at Tilsit.

historical_outcome.description: Crushing French victory. Russia sued for peace at Tilsit.

name: Battle of Wagram (1809)
description: A two-day engagement on the Marchfeld plain that broke Austrian resistance and ended the War of the Fifth Coalition.

historical_outcome.description: Costly French victory. Austria signed the Treaty of Schoenbrunn.

name: Battle of Aspern-Essling (1809)
description: Napoleon's first major defeat. Austrian forces caught the French crossing the Danube and inflicted a serious reverse.

historical_outcome.description: Austrian victory. Napoleon was forced to retreat back across the Danube.

name: Battle of Salamanca (1812)
description: Wellington exploited a gap in the French line and delivered a crushing blow to Marmont's Army of Portugal.

historical_outcome.description: Wellington's most complete battlefield victory.

name: Battle of Vitoria (1813)
description: Wellington's coordinated four-column assault shattered King Joseph's army and ended French rule in Spain.

historical_outcome.description: Decisive Allied victory. France lost Spain.

name: Battle of Dresden (1813)
description: Napoleon's last major offensive victory, routing a larger Allied army attacking his Saxon capital.

historical_outcome.description: French victory but strategically inconclusive.

name: Battle of Leipzig (1813)
description: The Battle of the Nations. Four Allied armies converged on Napoleon from all sides over three days.

historical_outcome.description: Catastrophic French defeat. Germany was liberated.

name: Battle of Ligny (1815)
description: Napoleon's last victory. The French defeated Blucher's Prussians two days before Waterloo.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Blucher was wounded but the Prussians remained a coherent force.

name: Battle of Quatre Bras (1815)
description: Ney's attack on Wellington's position at the Quatre Bras crossroads. Ultimately inconclusive.

historical_outcome.description: Tactically inconclusive. Wellington withdrew to his Waterloo position overnight.

name: Battle of Marengo (1800)
description: Napoleon nearly lost the battle until Desaix's corps returned just in time, a dramatic reversal.

historical_outcome.description: French victory after near-defeat. Desaix's counterattack reversed the battle.

name: Battle of Arcola (1796)
description: Three-day battle at the Alpone marshes. Napoleon's crossing of the Arcola bridge under fire.

historical_outcome.description: French victory after three days of fierce fighting across the marshes.

name: Battle of Rivoli (1797)
description: Napoleon defeated each Austrian column in detail as they arrived on the plateau.

historical_outcome.description: Decisive French victory. Austria's final attempt to relieve Mantua was crushed.

name: Battle of Smolensk (1812)
description: Napoleon hoped for a decisive battle at Smolensk, but Barclay de Tolly withdrew after bitter street fighting.

historical_outcome.description: Tactical French victory but strategic failure. Russia preserved her army.

name: Battle of Lutzen (1813)
description: Napoleon's first battle after the Russian disaster, fought with inexperienced conscripts.

historical_outcome.description: French victory but without decisive pursuit. Napoleon lacked cavalry.

name: Battle of Bautzen (1813)
description: Napoleon's flanking manoeuvre nearly trapped the Allied army, but Ney failed to arrive in time.

historical_outcome.description: French victory but without decisive pursuit.

name: Battle of Eckmuhl (1809)
description: Napoleon's rapid concentration and strike at the Austrian right flank, saving Davout's corps.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Archduke Charles withdrew across the Danube.

name: Battle of Talavera (1809)
description: Wellington repelled repeated French attacks on his position but could not destroy Victor's army.

historical_outcome.description: Allied defensive victory. Wellington earned his viscountcy.

name: Battle of Albuera (1811)
description: One of the bloodiest battles of the Peninsular War. Beresford's allied force defeated Soult's flanking attack.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory at tremendous cost. Both sides suffered horrific casualties.

name: Battle of the Berezina (1812)
description: The French Grand Armee's desperate crossing of the frozen Berezina River while surrounded.

historical_outcome.description: The core of the Grand Armee escaped, though with massive losses.

name: Battle of Hanau (1813)
description: Napoleon fought through Bavarian-Austrian forces blocking his retreat to France after Leipzig.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Napoleon cleared the road to France.

name: Battle of Montmirail (1814)
description: The centrepiece of Napoleon's Six Days campaign. He defeated Sacken's Russians and York's Prussians in succession.

historical_outcome.description: Brilliant French victory during the Six Days campaign.

name: Battle of Vauchamps (1814)
description: The final battle of Napoleon's Six Days campaign. Grouchy's cavalry cut off Blucher's retreating Prussians.

historical_outcome.description: Crushing French victory. The Six Days ended in triumph.

name: Battle of La Rothiere (1814)
description: Napoleon was overwhelmed by converging Allied forces and suffered a defensive defeat.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory. Napoleon was outnumbered 3:1.

name: Battle of Castiglione (1796)
description: Napoleon concentrated against Wurmser's dispersed columns and defeated each in turn.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Wurmser's relieving army was defeated.

name: Battle of Bassano (1796)
description: Napoleon struck Wurmser's retreating column at Bassano, capturing his artillery and forcing Wurmser into Mantua.

historical_outcome.description: Complete French victory. Wurmser was trapped in Mantua.

name: Battle of Novi (1799)
description: Suvorov's Austro-Russian forces decisively defeated the French Army of Italy.

historical_outcome.description: Decisive Allied victory. The French Army of Italy was shattered.

name: Battle of Trebbia (1799)
description: Suvorov's rapid march caught Macdonald's French army on the Trebbia River.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory over three days. Macdonald escaped with the remnants of his army.

name: Battle of Ulm (1805)
description: Napoleon's strategic encirclement trapped Mack's Austrian army without a pitched battle.

historical_outcome.description: French strategic masterpiece. 30,000 Austrians surrendered.

name: Battle of Auerstedt (1806)
description: Davout's single corps defeated the main Prussian army in the most astonishing feat of arms of the Napoleonic Wars.

historical_outcome.description: Extraordinary French victory. Davout's 26,000 defeated 50,000 Prussians.

name: Battle of Heilsberg (1807)
description: The French attacked a heavily fortified Russian camp at Heilsberg and were repulsed with heavy losses.

historical_outcome.description: Russian defensive victory. French frontal attacks on the entrenchments were repulsed.

name: Battle of Hohenlinden (1800)
description: Moreau's French army used the forest terrain to trap Archduke John's Austrians in a near-perfect ambush.

historical_outcome.description: Decisive French victory. Austria sued for peace.

name: Battle of Maloyaroslavets (1812)
description: Eugene's corps fought for control of Maloyaroslavets. Napoleon chose to retreat via Smolensk.

historical_outcome.description: Tactical French victory but strategic decision to retreat sealed the campaign's failure.

name: Battle of Wavre (1815)
description: Grouchy pinned Thielmann's Prussian corps at Wavre, failing to prevent Blucher from marching to Waterloo.

historical_outcome.description: French tactical victory at Wavre but strategic defeat at Waterloo.

name: Battle of Fuentes de Onoro (1811)
description: Wellington successfully defended the village of Fuentes de Onoro in two days of fighting.

historical_outcome.description: Allied defensive victory. Massena failed to relieve Almeida.

name: Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube (1814)
description: Napoleon's last offensive operation of the 1814 campaign, fighting Schwarzenberg's main army.

historical_outcome.description: French defeat. Napoleon retreated; the 1814 campaign was nearly over.

name: Battle of Tolentino (1815)
description: Murat's desperate offensive into central Italy was crushed by the Austrians.

historical_outcome.description: Austrian victory. Murat fled; Naples fell to the Bourbons.

name: Battle of Ratisbon (1809)
description: The decisive engagement of Napoleon's spring 1809 campaign. Napoleon was slightly wounded.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Charles's army crossed the Danube.

name: Battle of Lonato (1796)
description: Napoleon defeated Quosdanovich's Austrian advance guard before facing Wurmser at Castiglione.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. Quosdanovich's corps was routed.

name: Battle of Millesimo (1796)
description: Napoleon separated the Austrian and Piedmontese armies, opening the Italian campaign.

historical_outcome.description: French victory opened the Italian campaign.

name: Battle of Lodi (1796)
description: Napoleon's famous bridge crossing over the Adda river under Austrian fire.

historical_outcome.description: French victory. The road to Milan was open.

name: Battle of Paris (1814)
description: The Allied assault on Paris while Napoleon marched to its relief.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory. Paris capitulated; Napoleon abdicated on April 6.

name: Battle of Kulm (1813)
description: Allied forces surrounded and captured Vandamme's corps retreating from Dresden.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory. Vandamme and 10,000 troops were captured.

name: Battle of Dennewitz (1813)
description: Ney's attempt to march on Berlin was defeated by Bulow's Prussian corps.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory. Berlin was saved.

name: Battle of Brienne (1814)
description: Napoleon's first battle of the 1814 campaign, a surprise attack on Blucher's headquarters.

historical_outcome.description: Indecisive. Napoleon captured the castle briefly but was driven back.

name: Battle of Champaubert (1814)
description: The opening battle of the Six Days campaign. Napoleon caught and destroyed Olsufiev's isolated Russian corps.

historical_outcome.description: Complete French victory. Olsufiev's corps was annihilated; he was taken prisoner.

name: Battle of Bar-sur-Aube (1814)
description: Oudinot and Macdonald were defeated by Schwarzenberg's converging Austrians.

historical_outcome.description: Allied victory. The French were pushed back on Paris.

name: Battle of Saint-Dizier (1814)
description: Napoleon's final offensive manoeuvre of the 1814 campaign, attacking Allied communications.

historical_outcome.description: French tactical victory, but strategically irrelevant — Paris fell the same day.
